name: Diversity-Oriented Fine-Tuning for Uncertainty-Based Hallucination Detection
summary: arXiv:2607.16643v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing hallucination detection methods are typically conducted at the inference stage, without making any modifications to the model itself. In thi...
description: arXiv:2607.16643v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing hallucination detection methods are typically conducted at the inference stage, without making any modifications to the model itself. In this paper, we are interested in exploring fine-tuning strategies that enhance the detectability of hallucinations in the resulting model. Focusing on semantic-entropy-based detection, we observe that ma...
author: Qiuyuan Li, Hongliang Dai, Piji Li
